architecture.summary: SIGA is a lightweight adapter that wraps an off-the-shelf coding agent (TreeSim) to configure scientific simulators by supplying an executable contract through retrieval of example decks, procedural memory of past trajectories, agent-callable validation functions, and validation-gated termination. The coding agent remains frozen; only the adapter (retrieval index, memory store, validation rules) is external and mutable. Information flows: the agent queries the adapter for relevant examples and validation feedback, runs simulator commands, and terminates only when validation gates pass.

biblio.title: Auto-Configuring Scientific Simulators with Lightweight Coding-Agent Adapters

claims.statement: on harder held-out tasks SIGA improves TreeSim from 0.720 to 0.789
SIGA reduces across-run standard deviation by about 16x by preventing empty or invalid decks
in a human calibration, SIGA reaches in about five minutes the deck quality a domain expert reached in about three hours
SIGA's main gain is reliability on harder held-out tasks

transfers to OpenFOAM and LAMMPS show the recipe is portable but interface-dependent: completion gates help when structural completeness is the bottleneck, while memory and retrieval help when value correctness is
the adapter supplies the simulator's executable contract through retrieval, procedural memory, agent-callable validation, and validation-gated termination while leaving the model and loop frozen
prior trajectories can rewrite the adapter contents without modifying the underlying agent
